obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems placed on the Union market shall report any serious incident to the market surveillance authorities of the Member States where that incident occurred.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI serious incident reporting requirements, including serious incident identification criteria, market surveillance authority notification procedures, Member State jurisdiction determination, and reporting timeline compliance.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI serious incident reporting requirements, including serious incident identification criteria, market surveillance authority notification procedures, Member State jurisdiction determination, and reporting timeline compliance.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 73(1) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.605, cosine: 0.695). Secondary: AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.680), AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.653).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.605). Shared vocabulary: [ai, compliance, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [authority, criteria, determination, identification, incident, jurisdiction] vs WIDAI terms [act, assessment, categories, conformity, documentation, eu] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.892 (P=0.886, R=0.898). Balanced P/R — the 25-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.695 > STS 0.605 (gap +0.090). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, compliance, knowledge, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 19.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, compliance, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act-only: [authority, criteria, determination, identification, incident, jurisdiction]. WIDAI-only: [act, assessment, categories, conformity, documentation, eu].
